error.signature: AI generates or displays Nazi symbols or Holocaust-trivializing content in German/Austrian/EU contexts

verdict.summary: Germany, Austria, and several EU countries criminalize Nazi symbols, salutes, and Holocaust denial. §86a StGB (Germany) carries up to 3 years imprisonment. AI must never generate such content.

dead_ends.action: Generate images containing swastikas, SS runes, or other Nazi insignia
dead_ends.why_fails: §86a StGB criminalizes distribution of symbols of unconstitutional organizations. This includes digital content. Penalties: up to 3 years imprisonment or fine. No artistic exception for casual use.
dead_ends.condition: Any content generation for German/Austrian audience

dead_ends.action: Make comparative or trivializing statements about the Holocaust
dead_ends.why_fails: Volksverhetzung (§130 StGB) criminalizes Holocaust denial and trivialization. Statements like 'it wasn't that bad' or numerical comparisons are prosecutable. EU Framework Decision 2008/913/JHA extends this across EU.

dead_ends.action: Use Nazi-era terminology casually (Führer, Heil, Endlösung, etc.)
dead_ends.why_fails: These terms carry extreme weight in German-speaking countries. Using them casually (even in non-political contexts) causes immediate revulsion and can constitute Volksverhetzung depending on context.

workarounds.action: In historical/educational contexts, describe events factually without reproducing symbols. Use text descriptions ('Nazi-era flag') rather than visual reproduction. Academic exception exists but is narrowly applied.

For game/media design targeting German market: replace or remove Nazi imagery (as Wolfenstein, Hearts of Iron do for German releases). Use fictional substitutes if the narrative requires an antagonist regime.

When discussing this history, use measured, respectful language. Acknowledge German Vergangenheitsbewältigung (coming to terms with the past) as a serious cultural process, not something to be dismissed.
workarounds.condition: Cultural sensitivity in German historical context

frequently_confused_with.distinction: Both involve legal restrictions on speech/content but German law targets historical symbols while Thai law protects a living institution
